Class EfsEventBus

java.lang.Object
org.efs.bus.EfsEventBus
public class EfsEventBus extends Object
A publish-subscribe event bus implementation that manages type+topic-based event distribution between publishers (advertisers) and subscribers within a single JVM.

Overview:

EfsEventBus provides a decoupled communication mechanism where agents can:

  • Advertise the ability to publish events on specific type+topic keys or wildcard topic patterns for a given efs event type.
  • Subscribe to events on specific concrete type+topic keys or wildcard topic patterns for a given efs event type.
  • Route events dynamically based on event content

Key Features:

  • Concrete Topics: Specific topic subscriptions and advertisements.
  • Wildcard Topics: Pattern-based advertisements and subscriptions using regex matching.
  • Inbox Subscriptions: Conflation-based subscriptions delivering only the latest event.
  • Router Subscriptions: Dynamic event routing to different agents based on event content.
  • Status Tracking: Publisher and subscriber agents receive status updates when availability changes.
  • Thread-Safe: All operations are thread-safe using concurrent data structures

Usage Patterns:

Pattern 1: Basic Concrete Topic Publishing and Subscription

// Get or create the event bus
 EfsEventBus eventBus = EfsEventBus.findOrCreateBus("myBus");

 // Publisher side: Advertise ability to publish OrderEvent on topic "orders"
 EfsTopicKey<OrderEvent> topicKey = EfsTopicKey.getKey(OrderEvent.class, "orders");

 Advertisement<OrderEvent> advertisement = eventBus.advertise(
     topicKey,
     subscribeStatus -> {
         // Called when subscriber count changes
         System.out.println("Active subscribers: " + subscribeStatus.activeSubscribers());
     },
     publisherAgent  // IEfsAgent instance
 );

 // Enable publishing
 advertisement.publishStatus(true);

 // Publish an event
 if (advertisement.hasSubscribers()) {
     OrderEvent event = new OrderEvent("ORDER-123", 99.99);
     advertisement.publish(event);
 }

 // Close the advertisement when done
 advertisement.close();


 // Subscriber side: Subscribe to OrderEvent on topic "orders"
 Subscription<OrderEvent> subscription = eventBus.subscribe(
     topicKey,
     publishStatus -> {
         // Called when publisher availability changes
         System.out.println("Publishers available: " + publishStatus.activePublishers());
     },
     event -> {
         // Receive and handle events
         System.out.println("Received order: " + event.orderId());
     },
     subscriberAgent  // IEfsAgent instance
 );

 // Close subscription when done
 subscription.close();

Pattern 2: Inbox (Conflated) Subscription

Inbox subscriptions are useful when a subscriber is slow or inbound events have a bursty publish and subscriber only cares about the latest event value, not all intermediate events. 

// Subscribe to inbox instead of concrete subscription
 Subscription<SensorEvent> inboxSub = eventBus.subscribeInbox(
     sensorTopicKey,
     publishStatus -> {
         // Handle publisher status changes
     },
     sensorEvent -> {
         // Always receives the latest event value
         updateDisplay(sensorEvent);
     },
     slowSubscriber
 );

 // Fast publishers can publish many events
 // Subscriber will only receive the most recent one
 for (int i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {
     advertisement.publish(new SensorEvent(i));
 }

 inboxSub.close();

Pattern 3: Wildcard Topic Subscriptions

Wildcard subscriptions use regex patterns to match multiple concrete topics dynamically. When new topics matching the pattern are discovered, the subscriber is notified. 

// Subscribe to all sensor topics matching pattern "sensor/.*"
 WildcardSubscription<SensorEvent> wildSub = eventBus.subscribeAll(
     SensorEvent.class,
     "sensor/.*",           // Regex pattern
     publishStatus -> {
         // Handle publisher status
     },
     event -> {
         // Handle events from matching topics
         System.out.println("Sensor data: " + event);
     },
     topicKey -> {
         // Called when a new topic matching the pattern is discovered
         System.out.println("New topic discovered: " + topicKey.topic());
     },
     subscriberAgent
 );

 // Concrete subscriptions are automatically created as new topics appear
 // e.g., when publisher advertises "sensor/temperature",
        wildcard subscriber automatically subscribes

 wildSub.close();

Pattern 4: Wildcard Topic Advertisement

Wildcard advertisements allow a publisher to advertise for multiple concrete topics matching a regex pattern. 


 // Advertise ability to publish SensorEvent on all topics matching "sensor/.*"
 WildcardAdvertisement<SensorEvent> wildAd = eventBus.advertiseAll(
     SensorEvent.class,
     "sensor/.*",           // Regex pattern
     subscribeStatus -> {
         // Handle subscriber count changes
     },
     topicKey -> {
         // Called when a new topic matching the pattern is discovered
         System.out.println("Can now advertise to: " + topicKey.topic());
     },
     publisherAgent
 );

 // Set publish status for all matching topics
 wildAd.publishStatusAll(true);

 // Publish to all topics (subscribers to "sensor/temp", "sensor/humidity", etc.
    receive events as concrete advertisements are created)
 wildAd.close();

Pattern 5: Event Router Subscription

Router subscriptions dynamically route events to different agents based on event content. Useful for conditional event distribution among multiple agents of the same class, allowing for parallel event processing for independent events of the same type. 


 // Create a router that routes based on order priority
 IEventRouter<OrderEvent> router = event -> {
     if ("HIGH".equals(event.priority())) {
         return new EfsDispatchTarget<>(highPriorityAgent, priorityHandler);
     } else {
         return new EfsDispatchTarget<>(normalAgent, normalHandler);
     }
 };

 // Subscribe with router
 Subscription<OrderEvent> routerSub = eventBus.subscribeRouter(
     orderTopicKey,
     publishStatus -> {
         // Handle publisher status
     },
     router,
     masterAgent
 );

 routerSub.close();

Pattern 6: Managing Multiple Topics

There is a real problem with using wildcard subscriptions and advertisements which reference the same topics. Both publisher and subscriber are using regular expression patterns for topics but wildcard topic matching is only performed against concrete topics. Therefore, combined wildcard subscription and advertisement do not result in concrete subscriptions and advertisements being created.

When the above scenario is occurring in an application, the solution is to put the concrete topics into the event bus via addTopic(EfsTopicKey) or addTopics(Class, List). As these concrete topic keys are added, existing wildcard subscriptions and advertisements are matched against these keys. 


 // Register multiple concrete topics with the bus
 EfsTopicKey<OrderEvent> orders = EfsTopicKey.getKey(OrderEvent.class, "orders");
 EfsTopicKey<PaymentEvent> payments = EfsTopicKey.getKey(PaymentEvent.class, "payments");

 eventBus.addTopics(OrderEvent.class, Arrays.asList("orders", "orders/expedited"));
 eventBus.addTopics(PaymentEvent.class, Arrays.asList("payments", "payments/refunds"));

 // Or add single topic
 eventBus.addTopic(orders);

Thread Safety and Concurrency:

  • All public methods are thread-safe.
  • Internal use of ConcurrentHashMap and AtomicXXX for concurrent access.
  • ReentrantLock used for topic map modifications to ensure atomicity. Note that locks are not used for event publishing. Since bus advertisements and subscriptions are generally created and closed at application start and stop, respectively, this is considered an acceptable trade-off.
  • Multiple agents can publish/received events concurrently.

Error Handling:

  • NullPointerException thrown if required arguments are null.
  • IllegalArgumentException thrown if topic is null, empty, or blank.
  • IllegalStateException thrown if:
    • Advertisement/subscription is closed
    • Publish status is false when attempting to publish
    • Agent not registered with EfsDispatcher
    • No subscribers available when publishing

Statistics and Monitoring:

Both Advertisement and Subscription instances provide access to statistics:

  • EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.eventCount(): Total events published/received.
  • EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.latestEventTimestamp(): Latest event timestamp.
  • EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isPublished(): Returns true if there are any publishers currently publishing events for given type+topic key or there are any subscribers for type+topic key. In the first case, if false is returned, then subscribers will not be receiving any events for type+topic key. In the second case, a false return value means there not subscribers for type+topic key and publishers should not post events to the key.
  • EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isOpen(): Whether access point is open or closed.
Author:
Charles W. Rapp

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static final class 
    EfsEventBus.Advertisement<E extends IEfsEvent>
    An advertisement is an publishing agent's access point to a event class+topic feed.
    static class 
    EfsEventBus.Subscription<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Abstract base class for concrete, inbox, and router subscriptions.
    final class 
    EfsEventBus.WildcardAdvertisement<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Track concrete advertisements created when new topics for given event class are introduced to event bus.
    final class 
    EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription<E extends IEfsEvent>
    Tracks concrete subscriptions created when new topics for given event class are introduced to event bus.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    ADVERTISEMENT_CLOSED
    Attempting to interact with a closed advertisement results in an IllegalStateException with the message "advertisement is closed".
    static final String
    INVALID_BUS_NAME
    An invalid bus name results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "busName is either null, an empty string, or blank".
    static final String
    INVALID_REGEX
    An invalid regular expression results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "invalid regular expression \"%s\"" containing the invalid regular expression.
    static final String
    INVALID_TOPIC
    An invalid topic results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "topic is either null, an empty string, or blank".
    static final int
    MAX_REGEX_SIZE
    Wildcard advertisement and subscription regular expressions must be ≤ 500 characters.
    static final String
    NO_SUBSCRIBERS
    Attempting to publish an event while there are no subscribers to the topic results in an IllegalStateException with the message "no subscribers".
    static final String
    NULL_AGENT
    A null agent argument results in a NullPointerException with message "agent is null".
    static final String
    NULL_CALLBACK
    A null callback results in a NullPointerException with message "callback is null".
    static final String
    NULL_EVENT
    A null event argument results in a NullPointerException with message "event is null".
    static final String
    NULL_EVENT_CLASS
    A null event class results in a NullPointerException with message "eventClass is null".
    static final String
    NULL_ROUTER
    A null event router argument results in a NullPointerException with message "event router is null".
    static final String
    NULL_TOPIC_KEY
    A null topic key results in a NullPointerException with message "topicKey is null".
    static final String
    NULL_TOPICS
    A null topics list argument results in a NullPointerException with message "topics is null".
    static final String
    PUBLISH_STATUS_DOWN
    Attempting to publish an event while publish status is set to false results in an IllegalStateException with the message "publish status is down".
    static final String
    TOPIC_COMPLEXITY
    An overly complex wildcard topic results in an IllegalArgumentException with message "regexTopic is too complex".
    static final String
    UNREGISTERED_AGENT
    A publisher or subscriber not registered with a dispatcher results in an IllegalStateException with message "%s is not registered with a dispatcher" containing agent name.

  • Method Details

    • busName

      public String busName()
      Returns efs event bus name which is unique within JVM.
      Returns:
      unique event bus name.

    • advertisementCreationCount

      public int advertisementCreationCount()
      Returns current number of advertisements successfully created on this event bus. This is not equivalent to the number of advertisements currently open.
      Returns:
      number of advertisements successfully created.

    • subscriptionCreationCount

      public int subscriptionCreationCount()
      Returns current number of subscriptions successfully created on this event bus. This is not equivalent to the number of subscriptions currently open.
      Returns:
      number of subscriptions successfully created.

    • wildcardMatchCount

      public int wildcardMatchCount()
      Returns current number of topics successfully matched against all advertisement/subscription wildcards.
      Returns:
      current number of successful wildcard matches.

    • addTopic

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> void addTopic(EfsTopicKey<E> topicKey)
      Reports a event topic key to bus independent of an advertisement or subscription. This method is used when advertiseAll(Class, String, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent) and subscribeAll(Class, String, boolean, Consumer, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent) reference the same topic keys. Since it is not possible to match advertised and subscribed wildcard topics, it is necessary to report concrete topics separately.
      Type Parameters:
      E - event type.
      Parameters:
      topicKey - event topic key to be added.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if topicKey is null.
      See Also:
      • addTopics(Class, List)

    • addTopics

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> void addTopics(Class<E> eventClass, List<String> topics)
      Reports multiple topics for a given event class independent of an advertisement or subscription. This method is used when advertiseAll(Class, String, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent) and subscribeAll(Class, String, boolean, Consumer, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent) reference the same topic keys. Since it is not possible to match advertised and subscribed wildcard topics, it is necessary to report concrete topics separately.

      Exceptions are thrown before any topic key processing happens, meaning that no topic keys are added to topic key map when an exception is thrown.

      Type Parameters:
      E - event type.
      Parameters:
      eventClass - topic key class.
      topics - topic key topics.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either eventClass or topics is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if topics contains a null, empty, or blank topic. If thrown, then no topics were processed.
      See Also:
      • addTopic(EfsTopicKey)

    • advertise

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> EfsEventBus.Advertisement<E> advertise(EfsTopicKey<E> topicKey, Consumer<EfsSubscribeStatus<E>> sscb, IEfsAgent publisher)
      Advertises an event class, topic published by an agent, returning a closeable instance used to retract this advertisement when closed. If this is a new topic key, then finds all extant wildcard subscriptions which match this topic key and has them subscribe.

      Returned Advertisement is then used to set the publish status and publish events.

      Type Parameters:
      E - event class.
      Parameters:
      topicKey - advertise event class and topic.
      sscb - EfsSubscribeStatus event callback.
      publisher - agent advertising ability to publish to given topic key.
      Returns:
      an Advertisement instance that may be used to:
      • Set publish status for all concrete advertisements via EfsEventBus.Advertisement.publishStatus(boolean).
      • Retract the advertisement via EfsEventBus.Advertisement.close().
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either topicKey, sscb, or publisher is null.
      IllegalStateException - publisher is not registered with EfsDispatcher.
      See Also:
      • EfsEventBus.Advertisement
      • advertiseAll(Class, String, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • subscribe(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)

    • advertiseAll

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> EfsEventBus.WildcardAdvertisement<E> advertiseAll(Class<E> eventClass, String regexTopic, Consumer<EfsSubscribeStatus<E>> sscb, Consumer<EfsTopicKey<E>> topicUpdate, IEfsAgent publisher)
      Advertises an event class with a wildcard topic pattern, allows a publisher to publish events to all topics matching the pattern. Returns a closeable wildcard advertisement instance used to retract this advertisement and manage dynamically created concrete advertisements.

      This method enables a publisher to advertise its ability to publish events across multiple topics that match a regular expression pattern. As new concrete topics are added to the event bus that match the wildcard pattern, the publisher is automatically notified via the topicUpdate callback, and concrete advertisements are dynamically created for those topics.

      The method validates that the event class, regular expression topic pattern, event callback, and that the publisher agent is registered with the EfsDispatcher. Exceptions are thrown before any modifications to internal state occur.

      Upon creation, the returned wildcard advertisement finds all existing concrete topics in the event bus that match the regular expression pattern and creates advertisements for each matching topic.

      Once a wildcard advertisement is closed, all dynamically created concrete advertisements are automatically closed as well, and the wildcard advertisement cannot be re-opened.

      Type Parameters:
      E - efs event class.
      Parameters:
      eventClass - target efs event class.
      regexTopic - regular expression matched against existing concrete topics.
      sscb - EfsSubscribeStatus event callback.
      topicUpdate - newly discovered topic keys matching wildcard reported via this callback.
      publisher - agent advertising ability to publish to given topic key.
      Returns:
      a WildcardAdvertisement instance that may be used to:
      • Query all concrete topic keys and topics currently advertised via EfsEventBus.WildcardAdvertisement.advertisementKeys() and EfsEventBus.WildcardAdvertisement.advertisementTopics().
      • Retract the wildcard advertisement via AutoCloseable.close().
      –
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if eventClass, sscb, topicUpdate, or publisher is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if regexTopic is null, empty, or blank, or if regexTopic is not a valid regular expression pattern, or if the regular expression is > MAX_REGEX_SIZE.
      IllegalStateException - if publisher is not registered with EfsDispatcher.
      See Also:
      • EfsEventBus.WildcardAdvertisement
      • advertise(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • subscribeAll(Class, String, boolean, Consumer, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)

    • subscribe

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> EfsEventBus.Subscription<E> subscribe(EfsTopicKey<E> topicKey, Consumer<EfsPublishStatus<E>> pscb, Consumer<E> ecb, IEfsAgent subscriber)
      Subscribes to a concrete event class and topic, returning a closeable subscription instance. This subscription is for regular (non-inbox) event delivery, where all published events are delivered to the subscriber's callback.

      This method enables an agent to subscribe to all events published to a specific event class and topic combination. The agent receives each event as it is published by any advertiser of that topic. Events are dispatched to the subscriber's callback as they occur.

      When the subscription is created, the subscriber receives the current publisher status via the pscb callback, informing them of how many publishers are currently advertising on that topic. Whenever a publisher status changes (publishers join or leave), the subscriber is notified via this callback.

      For inbox-based subscriptions where only the latest event is desired, use subscribeInbox(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent) instead.

      The method validates that the topic key, callbacks, and subscriber are valid, and that the subscriber is registered with the EfsDispatcher. Exceptions are thrown before any modifications to internal state occur.

      Type Parameters:
      E - efs event class
      Parameters:
      topicKey - subscribe to this event class and topic.
      pscb - forward publisher status events to this callback.
      ecb - forward events to this callback.
      subscriber - agent subscriber.
      Returns:
      a Subscription instance that can be used to:
      • Query type+topic key via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.topicKey().
      • Check if the subscription is open via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isOpen().
      • Check publisher status via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isPublished().
      • Query event statistics via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.eventCount() and EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.latestEventTimestamp().
      • Retract the subscription via EfsEventBus.Subscription.close().
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if topicKey, pscb, ecb, or subscriber is null.
      IllegalStateException - if subscriber is not registered with EfsDispatcher.
      See Also:
      • subscribeInbox(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • subscribe(boolean, EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • EfsEventBus.Subscription

    • subscribeInbox

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> EfsEventBus.Subscription<E> subscribeInbox(EfsTopicKey<E> topicKey, Consumer<EfsPublishStatus<E>> pscb, Consumer<E> ecb, IEfsAgent subscriber)
      Subscribes to a concrete event class and topic, returning a closeable subscription instance. This subscription uses event conflation (inbox delivery), where only the latest published event is delivered to the subscriber, not every intermediate event.

      This method enables an agent to subscribe to events published to a specific event class and topic combination, with the behavior that only the most recent (conflated) event is delivered. This is useful when:

      • The subscriber is interested only in the latest state, not in every intermediate event.
      • The subscriber has limited processing capacity and cannot keep up with rapid event publishing rates.
      • Intermediate event values are not needed for the application logic.

      When the subscription is created, the subscriber receives the current publisher status via the pscb callback, informing them of how many publishers are currently advertising on that topic. Whenever the publisher status changes (publishers join or leave), the subscriber is notified via this callback.

      For regular subscriptions where all events are delivered, use subscribe(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent) instead.

      The method validates that type+topic key, callbacks, and subscriber agent, and that the subscriber agent is registered with the EfsDispatcher. Exceptions are thrown before any modifications to internal state occur.

      Type Parameters:
      E - efs event class.
      Parameters:
      topicKey - subscribe to this event class and topic.
      pscb - forward publisher status events to this callback.
      ecb - forward events to this callback.
      subscriber - agent subscriber.
      Returns:
      a Subscription instance that can be used to:
      • Query type+topic key via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.topicKey().
      • Check if the subscription is open via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isOpen().
      • Check publisher status via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isPublished().
      • Query event statistics via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.eventCount() and EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.latestEventTimestamp().
      • Retract the subscription via EfsEventBus.Subscription.close()
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if topicKey, pscb, ecb, or subscriber is null.
      IllegalStateException - if subscriber is not registered with EfsDispatcher.
      See Also:
      • subscribe(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • subscribe(boolean, EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • EfsEventBus.Subscription
      • ConflationEvent

    • subscribeAll

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription<E> subscribeAll(Class<E> eventClass, String regexTopic, boolean isInBox, Consumer<EfsPublishStatus<E>> pscb, Consumer<E> ecb, Consumer<EfsTopicKey<E>> topicUpdate, IEfsAgent subscriber)
      Subscribes to an event class with a wildcard topic pattern, returning a closeable wildcard subscription instance. This subscription allows an agent to subscribe to all concrete topics matching a regular expression pattern, with events delivered either as all events (concrete) or only the latest event (inbox).

      This method enables an agent to subscribe to multiple topics across an event class using a single regular expression pattern. As new concrete topics are added to the event bus that match the wildcard pattern, the subscriber is automatically notified via topicUpdate callback, and concrete subscriptions are dynamically created for those topics.

      The method supports two delivery modes controlled by the isInBox flag:

      • Concrete (isInBox=false): All published events on matching topics are delivered to the subscriber callback as they occur.
      • Inbox (isInBox=true): Only the latest (conflated) event on each matching topic is delivered, useful when the subscriber is interested only in the most recent state rather than every intermediate event.

      When the subscription is created, the subscriber receives the current publisher status via the pscb callback for each matching topic, informing them of how many publishers are currently advertising on that topic. Whenever the publisher status changes (publishers join or leave), the subscriber is notified via this callback.

      The method validates event class, callbacks, and subscriber, and that the regular expression pattern is compilable. The subscriber agent must be registered with EfsDispatcher. Exceptions are thrown before any modifications to internal state occur.

      Upon creation, the returned wildcard subscription finds all existing concrete topics in the event bus that match the regular expression pattern and creates subscriptions for each matching topic.

      Once a wildcard subscription is closed, all dynamically created concrete subscriptions are automatically closed as well, and the wildcard subscription cannot be re-opened.

      Type Parameters:
      E - event type
      Parameters:
      eventClass - subscribing to this event class.
      regexTopic - regular expression matched against event topics within event class.
      isInBox - true if this is an inbox subscription.
      pscb - forward publisher status events to this callback.
      ecb - forward events to this callback.
      topicUpdate - inform agent of newly discovered topic keys via this callback.
      subscriber - agent subscriber.
      Returns:
      a WildcardSubscription instance which can be used to:
      • Check if this is an inbox subscription via EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription.isInbox().
      • Query all concrete topic keys and topics currently subscribed via EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription.subscriptionKeys() and EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription.subscriptionTopics().
      • Check if the subscription is open via EfsEventBus.WildcardAccessPoint.isOpen()
      • Query wildcard pattern via EfsEventBus.WildcardAccessPoint.wildcardTopic()
      • Retract wildcard subscription via AutoCloseable.close().
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if eventClass, pscb, ecb, topicNupdate, or subscriber is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if regexTopic is null, empty, or blank, or if regexTopic is not a valid regular expression pattern.
      IllegalStateException - if subscriber is not registered with EfsDispatcher.
      See Also:
      • EfsEventBus.WildcardSubscription
      • subscribe(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • subscribeInbox(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • advertiseAll(Class, String, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)

    • subscribeRouter

      public <E extends IEfsEvent> EfsEventBus.Subscription<E> subscribeRouter(EfsTopicKey<E> topicKey, Consumer<EfsPublishStatus<E>> pscb, IEventRouter<E> router, IEfsAgent subscriber)
      Subscribes to a concrete event class and topic using a dynamic event router, returning a closeable subscription instance. This subscription allows events published to a specific topic to be dynamically routed to different agents based on the event's contents.

      This method enables an agent to subscribe to events on a specific topic and route those events to other agents or targets determined by an IEventRouter. Unlike standard subscriptions where events are dispatched directly to a callback, router subscriptions use the router to dynamically determine the target agent and callback for each event at publish time.

      The router is responsible for inspecting each event's contents and deciding where it should be dispatched. If the router returns null, the event is quietly not dispatched. This enables flexible, content-based event routing and fan-out scenarios where events need to be delivered to different subscribers based on their properties.

      When the subscription is created, the subscriber receives the current publisher status via the pscb callback, informing them of how many publishers are currently advertising on that topic. Whenever the publisher status changes (publishers join or leave), the subscriber is notified via this callback.

      The method validates that the type+topic key, callback, router, and subscriber are not null, and that the subscriber agent is registered with the EfsDispatcher. Exceptions are thrown before any modifications to internal state occur.

      Note that unlike regular subscriptions, router subscriptions do not require an event callback (ecb) because event delivery is handled through the router's IEventRouter.routeTo(IEfsEvent) method.

      That said, care must be taken if routing is based on the router's stored state because IEventRouter.routeTo may be called by multiple threads simultaneously. Be sure that implemented router is thread-safe.

      Type Parameters:
      E - event class
      Parameters:
      topicKey - subscribe to this topic key.
      pscb - publish status event callback.
      router - routes events to agents.
      subscriber - agent placing this subscription.
      Returns:
      a Subscription instance that may be used to:
      • Query the type+topic key via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.topicKey().
      • Check if the subscription is open via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isOpen().
      • Check publisher status via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.isPublished().
      • Query event statistics via EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.eventCount() and EfsEventBus.AccessPoint.latestEventTimestamp().
      • Retract the subscription via EfsEventBus.Subscription.close().
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either topicKey, pscb, router, or subscriber is null.
      IllegalStateException - if subscriber is not registered with EfsDispatcher.
      See Also:
      • IEventRouter
      • EfsDispatchTarget
      • subscribe(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • subscribeInbox(EfsTopicKey, Consumer, Consumer, IEfsAgent)
      • EfsEventBus.Subscription

    • findOrCreateBus

      public static EfsEventBus findOrCreateBus(String busName)
      Returns efs event bus associated with the given name. If there is no such event bus for given name, then a new event bus is constructed for that name.
      Parameters:
      busName - bus name. Must be unique within JVM.
      Returns:
      event bus for given name.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if busName is either null, an empty string, or blank.
